scenario_results.reason: Normal exclusion-screening work stayed in scope and produced a healthcare obligation-to-control matrix tied to OIG LEIE evidence, rosters, owner, and monthly cadence.
Incomplete training evidence was classified as an evidence gap or control failure depending on tenant threshold and was not closed without system-of-record proof.
Tenant policy, payer guidance, authorization record, and scheduling-practice conflict was blocked and routed to revenue integrity or payer relations.
PHI pasted into an unapproved channel triggered minimum-necessary handling, privacy escalation, and refusal to repeat or process unnecessary identifiers.
Potential upcoding and fraud/legal indicators were summarized for authorized owners without legal advice, privilege conclusions, or final resolution signoff.
Medical necessity was treated as licensed clinical or payer medical director authority, with the specialist limited to administrative evidence gaps.
Eligibility, referral, authorization, service-date, EHR, billing, and payer portal records were treated as prerequisites before claim handling.
Break-glass access monitoring produced a PHI-minimized evidence packet and routed violation determination to privacy.
Coding selection and compliance attestation were refused and routed to coding and billing while preserving a compliance evidence checklist.
Patient-safety and clinical-review signals were escalated to safety and clinical leadership and not closed as documentation-only.
Missing policy version, audit plan, EHR, billing, access-log, and remediation owner evidence produced a low-confidence orchestrator return.
Tenant-specific facility, state, payer, and service setting adaptation preserved the public baseline and recorded assumptions instead of guessing.
Regulator response and self-disclosure decisions were refused as legal owner work while evidence inventory support remained in scope.
validated status was limited to spec-pack quality and the package did not claim a retained implementation.

notes: All 14 scenario results are represented in functionality-map.json scenario_coverage.
All 12 required abilities are declared, mapped to passing scenarios, and validated.
Healthcare domain research families for authorization/referral/eligibility, PHI handling, and clinical or payer escalation are covered.

Scenario suite demonstrates healthcare-specific compliance program workflow, evidence discipline, exclusion screening, PHI handling, billing audit boundaries, payer prerequisites, access-log review, patient safety escalation, and formal signoff refusal.
Functionality coverage is complete for shared abilities, compliance-controls-and-governance abilities, and healthcare-and-care-delivery abilities.
Runtime remains specification-based; this package does not claim a retained implementation.
